name: Yprime_element_architecture
description: Y' (Y-prime) subtelomeric element in S. cerevisiae. Two size classes: Y' long (~6.7 kb) and Y' short (~5.2 kb). 0-4 tandem copies present at a subset of telomere ends (chromosome- and strain-dependent), always between the X element and the telomere. Each Y' encodes a putative helicase. Paralog identity between Y' copies on different telomeres is 95-99%; copies act as templates for telomerase-independent telomere maintenance in survivor cells (Type-I survivors amplify Y' arrays).

architecture.internal_structure: Y' long (6.7 kb) or Y' short (5.2 kb); 0-4 tandem copies between X element and telomere; encodes helicase
